newsSinceJun6: No material new fundamentals since June 6. AGM is tomorrow (June 10: board refresh, auditor, capital measures) - a known scheduled event. The EUR1B bond refinancing closed June 1 (cuts financing costs toward <EUR150M by 2028), the EUR570M Infineon non-optical sensor sale closes this quarter, and the 'slow and wide' micro-emitter optical-interconnect development agreement (Q1 prototype, no revenue before ~2030) were all disclosed earlier. Sell-side has chased the rerating (JPMorgan Overweight CHF23.60, Jefferies Buy CHF21). Today's -1.1% is noise.

sources.label: Stocks Today - ams OSRAM June agenda: EUR570M sensor sale, board refresh, AI photonics bet
TipRanks - ams-OSRAM calls 2026 AGM (governance, auditor, capital measures)

verdicts.rankRationale: No genuinely new information since yesterday's hold; the latency thesis (low expectations, balance-sheet scars) keeps eroding as deleveraging is executed in plain sight and major brokers pile in after +103% YTD, but nothing today changes the setup enough to re-rank.
verdicts.bull: Deleveraging is real and accelerating: bond refi closed June 1, Infineon EUR570M sale closes this quarter, FCF turned positive, and the micro-emitter optical-interconnect program gives free AI-photonics optionality.
verdicts.neutral: Everything driving the stock was disclosed before June 6; AGM tomorrow is procedural. The name still has sensing/photonics exposure but is mid-rerating, so residual latency is shrinking without yet being exhausted.
verdicts.bear: After a YTD double and JPM/Jefferies upgrades with tripled targets, the 'under-owned, scarred' premise is mostly gone; the AI interconnect bet has no revenue before ~2030 and consumer/auto optics cycles can still disappoint.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Closing of the Infineon sensor sale this quarter plus AGM capital measures cementing the deleveraged balance sheet.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding after the rerating - sell-side upgrades have made the recovery consensus, leaving little hidden alpha if Q2 (EUR725-825M guide) merely meets expectations.

verdicts.rankRationale: Already pushed down yesterday on the crowding/indirectness call; no new CPO-relevant news since June 6 to move again, and the known micro-emitter 'slow and wide' interconnect program (Q1 prototype, ~2030 revenue) modestly supports keeping it on the list at the bottom rather than cutting further.
verdicts.bull: The development agreement for micro-emitter-array optical interconnects is concrete evidence ams-OSRAM could become a datacom photonics supplier, and a cleaned-up balance sheet lets it fund that bet.
verdicts.neutral: It remains an optoelectronics generalist with CPO-adjacent capability, not a design-win holder in AI-scale connectivity; rank 119 already reflects that distance from the bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: No revenue from the interconnect program before ~2030, the partner is undisclosed, and the stock has already doubled on the restructuring story - the CPO option is being given credit it has not earned.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Naming the optical-interconnect development partner or an accelerated timeline that pulls datacom revenue forward from 2030.
verdicts.primaryRisk: The AI-photonics initiative stalls at prototype stage while incumbent CPO/silicon-photonics suppliers lock up the sockets, leaving AMS with no path into the bottleneck.
